title: Ratification britannique

To all and singular to wbom these Presents shall come, Grecting! Wubereas a Convention between Us, in respect of Our Dominion of Canada, and Our Good Friend the President of the French Republic, concerning the Rights of Nationals and Commercial and Shipping Ma…

pages.text:
George, be the Grace of God, of

Great Mritain, Ireland and tbe

Iritish Dominions beyono the

Seas lking, Defender of the faith.

Emperor of India, dc., dc., ac.

To all and singular to wbom these

Presents shall come, Grecting!

Wubereas a Convention between Us, in respect of Our Dominion of Canada, and Our

Good Friend the President of the French Republic, concerning the Rights of Nationals and Commercial and Shipping Matters, was concluded and signed at

Ottawa on the Twelfth day of May, in the year of

Our Lord One Thousand Nine Hundred and Thirty- three, by the Plenipotentiaries of Us and of Our said

Good Friend, duly and respectively authorised for that purpose, which Convention is, word for word, as follows:—

Tole, having seen and considered the

Convention aforesaid, have approved, accepted, and confirmed the same in all and every one of its Articles and Clauses, as We do by these Presents approve, accept, confirm, and ratify it, in respect of Our

Dominion of Canada, for Ourselves, Our Heirs and

Successors; engaging and promising upon Our Royal

Word that We will sincerely and faithfully perform and observe all and singular the things which are contained and expressed in the Convention aforesaid, and that We will never suffer the same to be violated by any one, or transgressed in any manner, as far as it lies in Our power. For the greater testimony and validity of all which, We have caused Our Great

Seal to be affixed to these Presents, which We have signed with Our Royal Hand.

Certified to be a true copy of the Convention between France and

Canada signed the 12th day of May, 1933, concerning the Rights of Nationals and Commercial and Shipping Matters.

Dated this 29th day of May, A. D., 1933

Inder- Secretary of Star or External Affair

Given at Our Court of St. James, the

Thirtieth day of June, in the year of Our Lord

One Thousand Nine Hundred and Thirty-three, and in the Twenty-fourth year of Our Reign.

Janger?

/e
